license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d8u9wv
title: Running VirSorter in iPlant Discovery Environment
author: Simon Roux

steps_raw.instruction: Login to the Discovery Environment. https://de.iplantcollaborative.org/de/ The Discovery Environment is the section of iPlant hosting VirSorter.
Click on the "Data" button on the left. a
Select a directory to work in. a
Click on "Upload" to place your data in the current directory. a
Enter a name and select the output directory for the analysis. a
Click section 1, select an input file and a database (i.e. with or without viromes). a

You can also select a fasta file of additional viral sequences and select the 'virome decontamination' mode, designed to identify viral sequences in datasets that are mostly viral (as opposed to mining of viral signal in microbial genomes or metagenomes). a This step is optional.
You will be notified when the job is submitted, when the job is running, and when the job is complete. a

Once the job is completed the output directory will include the main csv output file, listing all sequences detected as viral with their associated category and metrics, alongside the other results files stored into different sub-directories. a
The file can be opened in iPlant or downloaded for further analysis.
